title: coryphantha vivipara var. sneedii
intro: Coryphantha vivipara var. sneedii, also known as Sneed's Living Rock Cactus, is a rare and endangered species of cactus native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Coryphantha vivipara var. sneedii include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and stems are typically harvested and dried to create teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and applications.
